meta_title: Open-Air Cinema: Organising an Outdoor Screening
meta_desc: Everything you need for your open-air cinema: giant inflatable screens, turnkey packages, hire and accessories. French manufacturer since 1992 — free quote.
hero_title: Organising an open-air cinema, from start to finish
hero_desc: Giant screen, projection, sound, structure: Hallucine provides all the equipment you need for your open-air cinema screening — whether it’s for a festival, town hall, works council or private party. A French manufacturer, producing equipment designed for outdoor use since 1992.
photo_alt: Open-air cinema screening on a giant inflatable screen at Hallucine
tout_desc: From large screens to the smallest accessories, we provide everything you need for your event — whether you buy or hire.
c1_desc: From 3 to 24 metres, ultra-lightweight, inflated in a matter of minutes without the need for lifting equipment.
c2_desc: Screen, projector and sound all in one: a ready-to-use projection system, hassle-free.
c3_desc: A one-off event doesn’t always justify buying the equipment — hire it for the duration of the event.
c4_desc: Projectors, sound systems, counterweights, covers: everything you need for a successful screening.
how_title: How to organise your open-air cinema
s1_desc: Depending on the size of the audience and the space available. If you’re unsure, we’ll help you choose the right size.
s2_desc: Projection, sound and structure: an all-in-one package or a bespoke setup tailored to your venue.
s3_title: Install in just a few minutes
s3_desc: Quick inflation using a blower, with no need for a crane or complicated assembly. An 8-metre screen can be set up in under 15 minutes.
s4_desc: Everything is ready: all that’s left to do is screen the film. And we’ll be available if you need us.
q1: Which screen should you choose for an open-air cinema?
a1: The right screen depends on the distance between the audience and the screen, and on the number of spectators. Allow for approximately 1 metre of screen width for every 10 to 15 metres of viewing distance. Our screens range from 3 to 24 metres; we can help you choose the right one for your venue.
q2: How much does it cost to organise an open-air cinema screening?
a2: The budget depends on the screen size, the equipment (projection, sound) and whether you choose to buy or hire. Hiring a full set-up starts at around a few hundred euros for a small event. Request a free quote for an accurate estimate.
q3: Do you need a licence to screen a film outdoors?
a3: Yes: a public screening requires the film’s screening rights (obtained through a distributor or a specialist company) and, in most cases, permission from the local council to use the venue. As for the equipment, no special arrangements are required.
q4: Can we hire the equipment instead of buying it?
a4: Yes. For a one-off event, hiring is often a better option than buying. We offer screens and complete set-ups for hire for the duration of your event.
cta_desc: Tell us about your event — venue, audience, date — and we’ll provide you with a free, bespoke quote.
